'''Scott Cram''' (born 30 January 1977) is a former [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who played as a forward in the 1990s and 2000s. A Scotland international representative forward, he played his club football in Australasia's [[National Rugby League]] for the [[Illawarra Steelers]], and in the [[Super League]] for the [[London Broncos#1994–2005: Broncos and Super League|London Broncos]].

==Background==
Cram was born in [[Wollongong]], [[New South Wales]], Australia.

==Playing career==
Cram was named the [[1997 ARL season]]'s rookie of the year.<ref>{{cite web|title=Illawarra History|url=http://www.dragons.com.au/illawarra-history|work=dragons.com.au|publisher=[[St. George Illawarra Dragons]]|accessdate=11 August 2012|url-status=dead|archiveurl=https://web.archive.org/web/20120528165554/http://www.dragons.com.au/illawarra-history|archivedate=28 May 2012}}</ref> After moving to the United Kingdom he became a [[Scotland national rugby league team|Scotland]] international and represented them at the [[2000 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref name="The Teams: Scotland">{{cite web|url=http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/scotland.stm|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20001216174500/http://news.bbc.co.uk/sport/hi/english/static/in_depth/rugby_league/2000/world_cup/team_profiles/scotland.stm|url-status=dead|archive-date=16 December 2000|title=The Teams: Scotland|publisher=BBC News|date=1 January 2000|accessdate=29 February 2008}}</ref>
